message:
So, there I was, enjoying a sunny afternoon walk with my dog, Max. Just as we reached the park, my phone decided it was the perfect moment to die. No more music, no more maps, and definitely no way to check in with anyone. It was just me and Max, soaking in the moment—no distractions. 

I couldn’t help but chuckle at how dependent I had become on that little device. It made me realize the importance of being prepared for those tech hiccups. Whether it’s knowing your way around the neighborhood or having a few fun games to play with your pup, it’s good to have a backup plan. 

Ever had a moment where your tech let you down? What did you do instead?
